subject: [N5] 33 action items from 2025-09-29 external-remotely-good-careerspan
body:

**5. ### Key Decisions and Agreements**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**6. **Partnership Framework Established**: Remotely Good (Teresa's community) identified as pilot case for Careerspan's virtual recruiter model targeting micro-communities. Rationale: Teresa has built Social Impact SF community (100+ events aggregated) and needs monetization path through recruitment. Matters because: validates our thesis that micro-communities are undiscovered niche (Logan's insight) and provides ground-up case study.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**7. **Technical Infrastructure Already Deployed**: Careerspan provisioned "remotely good" code with 100-200 licenses already distributed. System ready to push job postings to Teresa's community (~dozen employer relationships, commensurate roles posted). Matters because: removes technical barriers, enables immediate activation once implementation details are finalized.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**8. **Warm Intro to Major League Hacking**: Vrijen offered connection to MLH founder (Amanda) for Teresa's November diversity/equity hackathon. Logan has call scheduled with MLH Wednesday 2025-10-02. Matters because: strengthens Teresa's event portfolio and positions Careerspan as valuable connector (relationship currency).**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Networking | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**9. ### Strategic Context**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**10. Teresa is navigating the classic community-to-business transition challenge: built engagement (Tech Week events, 100+ event calendar on Luma) but needs revenue model. Her context as "impact leader" (Jessica Greenwald's track) aligns perfectly with Careerspan's values-first positioning. She's experiencing founder overwhelm (four Tech Week event requests, learning to say no) which signals she's at inflection point where professional infrastructure (like Careerspan partnership) becomes critical.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**11. The meeting reveals Teresa as connector archetype—aggregates events, builds calendars, hosts mixers—which makes her ideal distribution channel. She's highly connected (Jessica Greenwald network, climate week connections, SF tech scene) but needs to convert social capital into business model. Our virtual recruiter model solves her exact problem: monetize community without building recruitment infrastructure herself.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Personnel | 🏷 meeting
   📝 From meeting recap...

**12. Competitive landscape hint: Teresa mentioned other people approaching her for Tech Week events, suggesting multiple parties recognize her community's value. We're early but not alone in seeing the opportunity.**
   📅 Wednesday 2:00pm | ⏱ 30m | 🟡 Normal
   📁 Operations | 🏷 none
   📝 From meeting recap...

**18. **Virtual Recruiter Pilot**: We positioned Remotely Good as ideal trial case for our micro-community model. Your "remotely good" code (100-200 licenses) is deployed and ready to push roles from our ~dozen employer relationships. Logan walked through implementation details—let us know if additional questions surfaced post-call.**
